cases.prompt: Review this wall panel draft: 'Halal Grill — 100% Halal Chicken. Every Bite, Certified.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL. Customer-facing surface cannot use the word 'halal'. Rewrite using supplier credential (Maple Lodge Farms) and remove absolute claim language.
assertions: flags 'halal' on wall / signage
references SKILL.md Section 8 halal handling rule
suggests 'Maple Lodge Farms chicken' as the supplier credential
removes '100%' absolute-claim framing

cases.prompt: Review this caption: 'In today\u2019s ever-evolving culinary landscape, Lazzat offers a vibrant tapestry of flavours — a seamless, unparalleled experience that will elevate your palate.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL. Contains Tier 1 AI words (landscape, vibrant, tapestry, seamless, unparalleled, elevate) and filler phrase. Rewrite with specificity and fragments per Section 17 voice rules.
assertions: flags at least four Tier 1 AI words
flags 'in today\u2019s ever-evolving' filler phrase
rewritten version contains one fragment under 5 words
rewritten version contains one specific number or proper noun

cases.prompt: Review this ad: 'Unlike Booster Juice, our shakes are made with real fruit — not syrups like the big chains use.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL. Names a competitor + implies competitor uses inferior ingredients. Reframe as information statement per Section 4.
assertions: flags 'Booster Juice' as a competitor name in customer copy
flags 'the big chains use' as an implied comparison
references Section 10 competitor-silence rule
suggests a 'what we can't serve our family' reframe

cases.prompt: Review this founder interview line: 'We built Lazzat because Osmow\u2019s had become too corporate — we wanted to do it right this time.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL. Violates the absolute no-disparagement rule on founders\u2019 existing restaurants (Section 9). Rewrite with learning-framed, forward-looking voice.
assertions: flags disparagement of Osmow\u2019s
references Section 9 absolute rule
rewritten version uses approved phrasing like 'twenty restaurants taught us...' or 'this is the first one that\u2019s mine'

cases.prompt: Review this wall panel: 'Our 850-million-year-old Himalayan salt wall releases negative ions and purifies the air as you dine, delivering 84 essential minerals for your wellbeing.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL. Wrong age figure, therapeutic claims (drug reclassification under FDA), 84-minerals misleading. Rewrite with the locked 600-million-year claim + 'naturally occurring minerals' only.
assertions: flags '850-million' as unsupported
flags 'negative ions / purifies the air' as therapeutic drug claim
flags '84 essential minerals'
references 600-million-year Khan et al. (2026) claim as the only verified figure
rewritten version uses 'approximately 600 million years' + 'naturally occurring minerals'

cases.prompt: Review this menu line: 'Try our fresh organic smoothies and hand-pressed juices — healthy, detoxifying, full of immunity-boosting nutrients.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL on multiple counts: 'smoothie', 'organic' without certification, 'juice', 'fresh' implied for frozen fruit, 'healthy / detoxifying / immunity-boosting' therapeutic claims.
assertions: flags 'smoothie' (use shake or fruit blend)
flags 'organic' without Canada Organic certification
flags 'juice' as a category (use fruit blend)
flags health claims (detoxifying, immunity-boosting)
suggests CFIA-defensible replacement language

cases.prompt: Review this caption: 'Chicken Tikka. 200 grams. Weighed before the grill. Every order. One of seventeen sauces, six continents, one table where everyone belongs.'
cases.expected_behavior: PASS. Contains specificity, a fragment, approved menu items (Chicken Tikka skewer name), approved numbers (200, 17), approved inclusivity phrasing. No competitor mention, no health claim, no banned AI words.
assertions: passes all seven tests in Section 18
approves 'Chicken Tikka' as a valid skewer name per Section 14.3 note
no rewrites required

cases.prompt: Review this delivery description: 'Our nut-free, gluten-free, allergen-free kitchen is safe for anyone with severe allergies.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL. 'Allergen-free' absolute claim is banned (Section 11). 'Gluten-free' without dedicated zone is risky - use 'gluten-friendly'. 'Safe for severe allergies' creates liability.
assertions: flags 'allergen-free' as absolute claim
flags 'gluten-free' without a dedicated zone (suggest gluten-friendly)
flags 'safe for severe allergies' liability wording
suggests 'Contains:' format and item-specific wording

cases.prompt: Review this hero line: 'Hormone-free chicken, raised the right way.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL. 'Hormone-free' is illegal framing in Canada (banned in poultry since 1963). Rewrite with 'raised without added hormones' plus required disclaimer, or pivot to supplier credential.
assertions: flags 'hormone-free' as illegal framing
references 1963 Canadian poultry law context
rewritten line uses 'Maple Lodge Farms chicken' or 'raised without added hormones' + disclaimer

cases.prompt: Apply the seven tests (Section 18) to this caption: 'Our grilled offerings feature premium quality ingredients, carefully selected by our talented team, delivering an unparalleled dining experience for our valued guests.'
cases.expected_behavior: FAIL on tests 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Specificity missing (no numbers, suppliers, named items). No fragments. Does not sound like a team member. AI Tier 1 words (unparalleled). Generic / corporate-filler tone.
assertions: identifies failure on test 1 (specificity)
identifies failure on test 2 (no fragment)
identifies failure on test 3 (who-wrote-this / corporate-sounding)
identifies failure on test 4 (Tier 1 AI word: unparalleled)
identifies at least one more banned phrase (premium quality, carefully selected, our talented team, valued guests)
provides a rewritten version that passes all seven tests
